items.title: Email classifier vs draft assistant
items.scenario: A bank deploys two ML systems: (A) flags phishing emails with a spam score, and (B) suggests reply text given a customer message thread.
items.explanation: System A is discriminative—one label per email. System B is generative—it produces novel text from a learned language model. Both are AI/ML; only B is Gen AI in the product sense.

items.title: Product photo vs synthetic catalog image
items.scenario: An e-commerce team uses a CNN to categorize uploaded photos (apparel type) and a diffusion model to create marketing images from text prompts.
items.explanation: Classification sits in classical supervised ML (often with deep nets). Image synthesis is Gen AI: the output is a new pixel array sampled from a generative model, not a category tag.

items.title: Same transformer stack, different product framing
items.scenario: One team fine-tunes a BERT-style encoder for sentiment on reviews; another uses a GPT-style decoder to continue product descriptions.
items.explanation: Both use deep learning and transformer blocks. The sentiment model is discriminative at deployment (score/label). The description model is generative (new tokens). Taxonomy is about the primary output, not whether "transformers" were used.
